identity.title_decision: Publication title renamed by author approval on 2026-05-26; source-attested title retained as provenance.

theory.canonical_statement: A local disharmonic fold is observed on an effective two-dimensional projected hypersurface derived from an unconstrained four-dimensional radial field; the projection selects a cylindrical zeroth-order Bessel profile.
theory.technical_interpretation: The source-recorded localized disturbance is completed here by an author-specified projection rule. This is an explicit model constraint, not the assertion that a literal coordinate slice of every isotropic four-dimensional solution changes its Bessel order.

canonical_equation.source_section_latex:
\[
\mathcal{L}_4 u_4=0,\qquad u_4(r)\propto\frac{J_1(k r)}{k r}
\]
\[
\mathsf{P}_{4\to2}:\mathcal{L}_4\rightsquigarrow\mathcal{L}_2,\qquad \delta\phi_{\mathrm{proj}}(r,t)=\varepsilon J_0(k r)\cos(\omega t)
\]
\[
J_0(k r_0)=0,\qquad k r_0=2.4048255577\ldots
\]

equation_progression.interpretive_role: The isotropic four-spatial-dimensional carrier supplies the higher-dimensional radial origin of the event.
The declared geometric constraint selects the effective two-dimensional radial sector used by the observed disturbance.
The projected mode gives the node value recorded by the resonance metric without misidentifying it as spherical Bessel j-zero.

derivation_layer.source_latex:
% BEGIN TZP DERIVATION LAYER
\tzpsection{Derivation Layer}
\paragraph{Unconstrained Four-Dimensional Mode.}
For a radially symmetric field on a four-spatial-dimensional slice,
\[
\mathcal{L}_4u_4=\left(\partial_r^2+\frac{3}{r}\partial_r+k^2\right)u_4=0,\qquad u_4(r)\propto\frac{J_1(k r)}{k r}.
\]
Its first boundary node satisfies \(J_1(k r^{(4)}_0)=0\), hence \(k r^{(4)}_0\approx3.8317059702\).
\paragraph{Hypersurface Projection Rule.}
Introduce the model constraint \(\mathsf{P}_{4\to2}\) selecting the effective two-dimensional longitudinal sector:
\[
\mathsf{P}_{4\to2}:\mathcal{L}_4\rightsquigarrow\mathcal{L}_2=\partial_r^2+\frac{1}{r}\partial_r+k^2.
\]
The projected localized fold therefore obeys
\[
\delta\phi_{\mathrm{proj}}(r,t)=\varepsilon J_0(k r)\cos(\omega t),\qquad J_0(k r_0)=0,\quad k r_0\approx2.4048255577.
\]
\paragraph{Interpretation.}
The higher-dimensional event remains hyperspherical in origin; its printed nodal metric is the effective projected observation selected by the declared constraint. This completes the node convention as a model-conditional extension of the sourced entry.
% END TZP DERIVATION LAYER

source_repair_review.basis: The extracted source supplies the radial disturbance and node value near 2.405 under its source-attested Drop Operator title; the author-approved Fold Operator title and P4to2 effective projection supply the cylindrical J_0 interpretation that makes that value consistent.

review_source_enrichment.method: equation-aligned source evidence plus author-specified P4to2 geometric constraint

symbol_definitions.\\mathcal{L}_4,\\mathcal{L}_2: four-dimensional and projected two-dimensional radial Helmholtz operators
symbol_definitions.\\mathsf{P}_{4\\to2}: declared hypersurface projection selecting the effective two-dimensional radial sector
symbol_definitions.\\omega: angular frequency of the projected oscillation
symbol_definitions.J_0(k r): cylindrical zeroth-order Bessel profile on the effective projected slice
symbol_definitions.k: radial wavenumber of the projected disturbance
symbol_definitions.r_0: first-node radius of the projected Bessel profile

dictionary_sections.definition: The disharmonic fold operator is a localized departure from coherent background behavior observed in an effective two-dimensional hypersurface sector. Its projected radial component is modeled by the ordinary zeroth-order Bessel profile \(J_0(k r)\), while its higher-dimensional carrier begins as an unconstrained four-dimensional radial wave.
dictionary_sections.contextual_meaning: The entry keeps the disturbance hyperspherical in origin without forcing its observable node metric to be a four-dimensional boundary zero. An explicit projection constraint \(\mathsf{P}_{4\to2}\) selects the two-dimensional longitudinal observation sector, where concentric radial rings naturally carry the required \(J_0\) node.
dictionary_sections.formal_statement_latex:
\[
u_4(r)\propto\frac{J_1(k r)}{k r},\qquad
\mathsf{P}_{4\to2}:\mathcal{L}_4\rightsquigarrow\mathcal{L}_2,\qquad
\delta\phi_{\mathrm{proj}}(r,t)=\varepsilon J_0(k r)\cos(\omega t)
\]
\[
J_0(k r_0)=0,\qquad k r_0\approx2.4048255577
\]
dictionary_sections.derivable_consequences: The unconstrained four-dimensional mode has first boundary zero at \(k r\approx3.8317059702\), whereas the projected two-dimensional mode has first zero at \(k r\approx2.4048255577\). The projection rule therefore converts a higher-dimensional origin into the nodal scale recorded for the localized fold.
dictionary_sections.lemma_support: The retrieved source supplies the local disturbance and records its node near 2.405. The hypersurface-projection derivation is an author-specified model extension: it resolves the previously inconsistent spherical notation by making the displayed node explicitly cylindrical \(J_0\) in the projected sector.
dictionary_sections.technical_interpretation: This is a controlled dimensional-reduction model. The operator \(\mathsf{P}_{4\to2}\) must be understood as a declared effective constraint or observation rule, not merely as evaluation of an isotropic four-dimensional solution on a plane. Under that rule, the radial equations and node values are consistent.
dictionary_sections.equation_grounding: \(u_4\) is the unconstrained four-dimensional radial carrier; \(\mathsf{P}_{4\to2}\) is the effective projection constraint; \(\delta\phi_{\mathrm{proj}}\) is the observed local phase deviation; \(J_0\) is the projected cylindrical Bessel profile; and \(r_0\) is its first-node radius.
dictionary_sections.interpretive_role: This entry links the higher-dimensional metric arc to a measurable projected resonance scale: the field event may originate in the hyperspherical sector while the recorded nodal pattern is evaluated in its effective planar observation layer.

tzp_thesaurus.meaning: A local departure from harmonic coherence represented as a structured disturbance.
The effective dimensional-reduction constraint selecting the observed radial sector.
The first radius at which the projected ordinary Bessel profile reaches zero.

review_publication_overrides.equation_support_summary: The explicit projection \(\mathsf{P}_{4\to2}\) maps the four-dimensional radial carrier to an effective two-dimensional disturbance profile, for which \(J_0(k r_0)=0\) gives \(k r_0\approx2.4048255577\).
review_publication_overrides.proof_latex:
\textbf{Statement:} Under the declared projection constraint,
\[
\mathsf{P}_{4\to2}:\mathcal{L}_4\rightsquigarrow\mathcal{L}_2,\qquad \delta\phi_{\mathrm{proj}}(r,t)=\varepsilon J_0(k r)\cos(\omega t).
\]
\textbf{Logic Validation:}\\
\textbf{Proposition:} The effective projected profile realizes the recorded first-node scale.\\
\textbf{Proof:} The regular 4D radial Helmholtz solution is proportional to \(J_1(k r)/(k r)\). By the stated model constraint, the observed sector obeys the 2D radial operator and has regular solution \(J_0(k r)\). Hence \(J_0(k r_0)=0\) gives \(k r_0=2.4048255577\ldots\).\\
\textbf{VERDICT:} \ensuremath{\checkmark}\ \textbf{TRUE UNDER PROJECTION CONSTRAINT}\\
\textbf{Formal Status:} THEORETICAL -- author-approved geometric extension in the production source truth\\
\textbf{Physical Status:} THEORETICAL -- projected realization remains to be tested

node_convention_review.source_recorded_claim: hyperspherical disturbance with first observed node near k r = 2.405
verified_relations: The regular unconstrained 4D radial mode is proportional to BesselJ[1, k r]/(k r) and has first boundary zero approximately 3.8317059702.
The effective projected 2D radial mode is BesselJ[0, k r] and has first zero approximately 2.4048255577.
node_convention_review.model_condition: P4to2 is an explicit effective radial-sector projection/constraint; it is not inferred from literal coordinate restriction alone.
node_convention_review.required_decision: Approved by author for production inclusion on 2026-05-26.
